subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the central theme of perseverance and persistence
Identify the rhyming scheme and simple poetic devices
Learn to overcome the fear of failure
Recognize how effort leads to success over time
Develop recitation skills and vocabulary related to effort
Connect the poem's message to real-life personal challenges

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Persistence
topics_covered: Introduction to the poet W.E. Hickson
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on things that were hard to learn (cycling, swimming)
Relating the title to everyday struggles
Brainstorming: What happens if we stop trying?
student_activities: Share a personal story of a time they failed but eventually succeeded
Predict what advice the poet will give

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Recitation
topics_covered: Reading the poem with correct intonation
Understanding the literal meaning of stanzas
Identifying key phrases like 'Tis a lesson'
teaching_strategy: Choral recitation (reading together as a class)
student_activities: Recite the poem in small groups
Underline new words and find their meanings in the glossary

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Message
topics_covered: The idea of 'disgrace' vs. 'glory' in failure
teaching_strategy: Discussion: Is it shameful to lose if you tried your best?
student_activities: Draw a 'Success Mountain' showing steps of trying
Answer simple 'True or False' questions based on the poem’s advice

lesson_plan.title: Poetic Structure
topics_covered: Repetition as a tool for emphasis
teaching_strategy: Highlighting rhyming pairs on the board
Explaining why the poet repeats the phrase 'Try Again'
student_activities: Find and list all rhyming pairs in the poem
Count how many times the phrase 'Try Again' appears

lesson_plan.title: Moral and Application
topics_covered: The universal truth of the poem
teaching_strategy: Discussion on 'If other folks can do it, why, with patience, may not you?'
Encouraging a positive mindset toward mistakes
Writing a collective 'Class Motto' based on the poem
student_activities: Write 2-3 lines on what they will do next time they face a hard math problem
Share one 'Patience' goal with the class

lesson_plan.title: Review and Fun Quiz
teaching_strategy: Word search or crossword puzzle based on the poem
Revision of the poem’s main advice

teaching_aids: Chart paper with the poem written in large letters
Audio recording of a professional poem recitation
teacher_tip: Make this lesson very encouraging. Praise the 'effort' during the activities more than the 'correctness' to stay true to the poem's theme. Remind students that even the best readers started by 'trying again' with the alphabet.
